content.full_text:
Assembly California Legislature

ROBERT J. CAMPBELL  
ASSEMBLYMAN, ELEVENTH DISTRICT

CHAIRMAN  
WAYS & MEANS SUBCOMMITTEE ON EDUCATION

January 12, 1990

Ms. Margaret Ellison  
Legal Division  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Ellison:

Thank you for your prompt verbal opinion on January 11, 1990 by phone. This letter is to confirm my understanding of that conversation and to request written verification of the opinion.

The situation resulting in the opinion request was a 90 day past due bill to Assembly Rules Committee for the purchase of a copy machine for a legislative district office. In order to prevent additional late charges and repossession of the machine by the vendor, Assemblyman Campbell is willing to pay for the machine for the district office from his officeholders campaign account.

The question is, "can a member pay a bill for the purchase of equipment for a state assembly office from his current officeholder account and be reimbursed by Rules Committee from the member's state assembly account?" The equipment is to be used for state public service only in a state assembly office.

At present it is my understanding that Rules will not pay late charges and at present, the late charges stand at $66.00 which must be paid by the member's officeholder account. Thanks to your prompt opinion by phone to approve payment from the officeholder account to the vendor and reimbursement by Rules, repossession proceedings have stopped and additional late fees will not incur.

My sincere thanks for your response particularly since Assemblyman Campbell is out of town on state business. I was unable to explain the circumstances to him and had to make a decision on his behalf.

Sincerely,

MariaTheresa Viramontes  
District Operations
